revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Fukagawa Fudō-dō]][[ja:成田山東京別院深川不動堂]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Fukagawa Fudodo Old Main Hall.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qid=Q674374|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}}|prefecture={{ill|Tomioka|qid=Q11456868|1=Tomioka}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.672917|N|139.798333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|qid=Q337624|1=Acala}}|part_of={{ill|Kantō 36 Fudō Pilgrimage|qid=Q11655872|1=Kantō 36 Fudō Pilgrimage}}}}
{{Overly detailed}} {{infobox religious building |name=Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|native_name=成田山東京別院深川不動堂|native_name_lang=ja|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}|image=Fukagawa Fudodo 03.JPG|thumb|caption=[[Honden|Main hall]] |map_type=Japan|coordinates={{coordinates|35|40|22.5|N|139|47|54|E|type:landmark_region:JP}}|tradition=[[Shingon]]|festivals=Goma Fire Ritual|festival=Goma Fire Ritual|municipality={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}|country={{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}|consecration_year=1703<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />|type=Branch Temple|year_completed=1703<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />|consecration_year=1703}}{{ill|Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|en:draft|Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|qq=}} (成田山東京別院深川不動堂) is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. It is famous for its ultra modern temple design and {{ill|Homa (ritual)|lt=Goma fire rite|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homa (ritual)|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-08-09 |title=Fukagawa Fudo-do Temple - Koto, Tokyo |url=https://en.japantravel.com/tokyo/fukagawa-fudo-do-temple/59960 |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=JapanTravel |language=en}}</ref><ref name="shitamachi-2017">{{Cite web|last=shitamachi|first=old tokyo|date=2017-05-05|title=Fukagawa Fudō-dō - Esoteric Tokyo|url=https://old-tokyo.info/fukagawa-fudo-esoteric-tokyo/|access-date=2024-03-29|website=Exploring Old Tokyo|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="japandeluxetours.com">{{Cite web|title=Fukagawa Fudō-dō {{!}} Fire Ritual {{!}} Tokyo {{!}} Japan Deluxe Tours|url=https://japandeluxetours.com/experiences/fukagawa-fudo-temple-tokyo|access-date=2024-03-29|website=japandeluxetours.com}}</ref> It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|qid=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} founded in 1703.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" /> Fukagawa Fudō dō is part of the {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|lt=Chisan school|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=}}. A subgroup of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}}. This temple focuses on the teachings of {{ill|Vajrayana|en|Vajrayana|qid=Q172175|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Vajrayana}}.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" /> [[File:門前仲町_shrine_secondary_building_古いお札納め所.jpg|thumb|A place for returning old talismans at {{ill|Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}]] The temple grounds feature structures. The main wooden building, visible from the front was originally built in {{ill|1862|en|1862|qid=Q7698|lt=1862|1=1862}}. Later moved to its location in {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}} after it didn't survive bombing during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. It previously served as the hall until a new modern hall was constructed in 2011. The modern hall stands out with {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|qid=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} inscriptions in black, white and gold. Another building known as the building was finished in 2000. Accommodates various temple activities.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />
Fukagawa Fudō dō is famous for its {{ill|Homa (ritual)|lt=Goma fire rite|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homa (ritual)|qq=}}, which involve burning sticks called gomagi. These ceremonies take place times a day. Are integral, to the spiritual practices of the temple. The purpose of these rituals is to summon [[Acala|Fudōmyō]] ō the deity of justice who wields a sword to ward off forces. Worshippers play drums during the rituals.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />
There are cermonies every day at 9:00 am, 11:00 am, 3:00 pm, and 5:00 pm, and every month at the first, fifteenth, and 28th day, there is an additional ritual held at 7 pm.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" /><ref name="Dominguez-2018" />
The Temple has many things on its grounds. In the hall a prayer gallery showcases 10,000 small crystal [[gorintō]] (five ringed towers). Inside the building rests the Onegai Fudōson statue skillfully crafted from 500 year old camphor wood sourced from Kumamoto Prefecture. The inner building features floors to visitors housing a statue of Fudōmyō ō created by sculptor Sawada Sekō, an immersive experience replicating the Shikoku O henro pilgrimage journey and a ceiling painting depicting {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Nyorai|qq=}} by painted by the famous painter {{ill|Chinami Nakajima|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinami Nakajima|qq=}}.<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />
The temple provides sutra writing training (shakyō) for a fee. This practice involves writing of characters or drawing images of Fudōmyō (shabutsu).<ref name="shitamachi-2017" />
== History == Fukugawa Fudo Temple can be found in the Koto ku region of Tokyo. One of its traditions is the {{ill|Homa (ritual)|lt=Goma fire rites|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homa (ritual)|qq=}} held in the main hall. The temple has a history dating back, to the century when Edo, now Tokyo was thriving. Despite losing its hall during World War II bombings a new hall was brought from Chiba in 1892 and stands as Tokyos oldest wooden building in Koto ward.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
== Goma Fire Rites == [[File:深川不動に竜が住む_Dragons_are_living_at_Fukagawa-fudo_temple._-_panoramio.jpg|thumb|The triple-dragon fountain people throw their wishes into]] The {{ill|Homa (ritual)|lt=Goma fire rite|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homa (ritual)|qq=}} is an Esoteric Buddhist ceremony performed to replenish the power of Fudōmyō-ō, enabling the deity to burn away the obstacles blocking your journey to {{ill|Enlightenment|en|Enlightenment|qid=Q15042830|lt=Enlightenment|1=Enlightenment}}. Attendees are encouraged to write down a {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|qid=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}} and throw their paper it into the turbulent water at the three dragons shrine fountain before entering the main hall for the ritual. During the ceremony, participants remove their shoes, and wooden sticks along with incense are burned in a sacred fire pit. Monks, some playing handheld taiko drums and blowing conch shells, recite Buddhist mantras in unison. The ritual involves people lighting sticks of incense on fire the blessing of personal belongings by holding them over the flames.<ref name="Dominguez-2018">{{Cite web|last=Dominguez|first=Kacey|date=2018-08-08|title=Tokyo's Fukagawa Fudo Temple Fire Ritual|url=https://www.wandering-traveler.com/fukagawa-fudo-temple/|access-date=2024-03-29|website=Wandering Traveler|language=en-US}}</ref>
These rituals take place five times a day from 9 a.m. To 5 p.m. with sessions on the first, fifteenth and twenty-eighth of each month including an additional ceremony at 7 p.m. for people who work late hours and are unable to make the other ceremonies.. Participants engage in a purifying act by burning cedar sticks in fire offering prayers to Fudōmyō ō, the justice deity. The ceremony lasts about half an hour. Includes drumming, chanting and meditation for peace.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
The {{ill|Homa (ritual)|lt=Goma fire rite|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homa (ritual)|qq=}} is held daily at 9:00 am, 11:00 am, 3:00 pm, and 5:00 pm, and every month at the first, fifteenth, and 28th day, there is an additional ritual held at 7 pm. The ceremony lasts approximately 45 minutes.<ref name="Dominguez-2018" /><ref name="shitamachi-2017" />
Visitors can join these rituals by paying an admission fee. Are reminded not to take photographs during the ceremonies or on temple grounds out of respect, for tradition.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
== Facilities ==
=== Treasures === The temple boasts treasures on its grounds, such as a fountain in the shape of three dragons spitting out water instead of fire. <ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />Attendees are encouraged to write down a {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|qid=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}} and throw their paper it into the turbulent water at the three dragons shrine fountain before entering the main hall for the Goma ritual. <ref name="Dominguez-2018" /> The temple has a remarkable collection of crystal figures depicting the deity Fudōmyō ō, who is highly revered at the temple. Additionally it serves as a traffic temple where locals come to seek blessings for their vehicles evident, from the forecourt filled with cars awaiting blessings. Visitors also have the chance to purchase charms for travels while driving.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
=== main hall === The Temple's New main hall Temple was built in 2011. Stands out with its black and gold Sanskrit inscriptions. This building is where the Goma ritual ceremonies and precious artifacts are kept.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
On the ground level theres a statue of Fudomyo o crafted by the talented sculptor Sawada Seko along, with a prayer area adorned with 10,000 crystal figures of Fudomyo o and a chamber showcasing intricate wooden deity carvings. Moving up to the floor it's all about the Shikoku O Henro theme representing the revered 88 Shikoku Temple Pilgrimage. This level is illuminated with LED lights and special effects suggesting that offering prayers here holds significance to completing the pilgrimage itself. Finally on the floor there's a mesmerizing ceiling painting depicting Dainichi Buddha created by Nakajima Chinami. All this temple stands as a must visit attraction, for tourists exploring Tokyo.<ref name="japandeluxetours.com" />
==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Approach to Fukagawa Fudo-do 201409.jpg|alt=|Approach to Fukagawa Fudo-do File:Fukagawa Fudodo 01.JPG|alt=|Fukagawa Fudodo Former main hall File:Fukagawa Fudodo Old main hall.jpg|Fukagawa Fudodo Former main hall File:Fukagawa Fudodo 03.JPG|alt=|Fukagawa Fudodo New main hall from the right File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05153.jpg|alt=|Old Temple with lanterns File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05157.jpg|alt=|Chozuya File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05163.jpg|alt=|Statue File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05167.jpg|alt=|Statue File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05170.jpg|alt=|Altar Statue File:Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05176.jpg|alt=|drums File:Fukagawa Fudoson 1 - Feb 12, 2012.jpg|alt=|Placard File:Fukagawa Fudoson 2 - Feb 12, 2012.jpg|alt=|donor plaque File:Fukagawa Fudoson 2019-1.jpg|alt=|main hall view inside File:Fukagawa Fudoson 2019-2.jpg|alt=|main hall view inside File:Fukagawa Fudoson 3 - Feb 12, 2012.jpg|alt=|closeup view from old hall File:Fukagawa Fudoson 4 - Feb 12, 2012.jpg|alt=|view of the street File:Fukagawa Fudouson 002.jpg|alt=|Old main hall next to new one under construction File:Fukagawa Fudouson.jpg|alt=|torii from ninjou street File:Fukagawa ninjou street.jpg|alt=|Fukagawa Ninjou Street File:Fukagawafudo20111228.jpg|alt=|main hall from the left File:Fukagawafudoson.jpg|alt=|Fukagawafudoson File:Shrine at Fukagawa Fudoson - Tokyo, Japan - DSC05159.jpg|alt=|Inari Shrine File:七五三 advertisement 2.jpg|alt=|Shichi-go-san advertisement File:深川不動に竜が住む Dragons are living at Fukagawa-fudo temple. - panoramio.jpg|alt=|Dragons at Fukagawa-fudo Temple File:門前仲町 shrine 3.jpg|alt=|former main hall File:門前仲町 shrine during 七五三 2.jpg|alt=|entrance during Shichi-Go-San File:門前仲町 shrine secondary building 古いお札納め所.jpg|alt=|Amulet return File:門前仲町 shrine secondary building.jpg|alt=|Monzen-Nakacho Shrine Secondary Building </gallery>
==See Also==
* [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] a shrine that also worshipped [[Acala]]

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Fukagawa Fudoson}} {{wikidata link|Q11495650}} [[Category:1703 establishments]] [[Category:1703 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:Acala]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Japan]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tokyo]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:Kanto 36 Fudo Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinshoji]] [[Category:Temples in Koto Ward]] [[Category:Temples of Fudō Myōō]] [[Category:Temples of Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
